rationale: The citing paper’s core model is a structural Bayesian learning choice model with quality uncertainty, transferred priors, and learning from experience, and the cited paper is also a structural Bayesian brand-choice-under-uncertainty model centered on prior uncertainty, learning, and perceived risk. That thematic/method overlap suggests the citation may be methodological or foundational rather than incidental. Even though the raw evidence is only a DOI match (no local citation context), this is not safely an obvious mention-only case.
